run.authorizationBasis: Repo owner confirmed in session; demo app is localhost-only.

summary.postureStatement: NOT ship-ready. The vulnerable build exposes auth bypass, unauthenticated admin access with credential leakage, and multiple injection points. The hardened build (demo-app/hardened/server.js) remediates all findings; live red-team confirmed every issue is blocked there.
summary.residualRisk: At Standard tier this covers the OWASP Top 10 for this app surface. Not covered: supply-chain hardening, infra/IaC, and advanced/APT scenarios — choose Hardened/Maximum for those.

findings.title: SQL injection in POST /login
findings.description: The username is concatenated directly into the SQL string, so an attacker can alter the query logic and bypass authentication.
findings.impact: Full authentication bypass and arbitrary data read/modification.
findings.evidence: Live: POST /login with username "' OR '1'='1' --" returned 200 {"ok":true,"user":"alice"} without a valid password.
findings.remediation: Use a parameterized query (WHERE username = ?) and verify the password hash separately (see hardened/server.js).

findings.title: Unauthenticated admin endpoint exposes users and password hashes
findings.description: The admin route has no authentication or role check, and its response includes the pwd_hash column, leaking credential material.
findings.impact: Any anonymous user can enumerate all accounts and obtain password hashes for offline cracking.
findings.evidence: Live: GET /admin/users with no credentials returned 200 with all users including pwd_hash values.
findings.remediation: Require authentication + admin role; never select/return pwd_hash. See hardened/server.js (authRequired + adminRequired, hash omitted).

findings.title: IDOR: any note readable without authorization
findings.description: Notes are returned by id with no authentication or ownership check, so any user can read any other user's notes.
findings.impact: Cross-account data disclosure of private notes.
findings.evidence: Live: GET /notes/1 and /notes/2 returned full note objects with zero authentication.
findings.remediation: Require auth and verify the note's owner matches the caller (see hardened/server.js ownership check).

findings.title: Second-order SQL injection in GET /notes/:id
findings.description: The :id path parameter is concatenated into the SQL query, giving a second injection point distinct from the access-control flaw.
findings.impact: Query manipulation / data exfiltration via the notes lookup.
findings.evidence: Code review: `WHERE id = ${req.params.id}` builds SQL from untrusted input with no parameterization.
findings.remediation: Parameterize and coerce id to an integer (see hardened/server.js: Number(id) + WHERE id = ?).

findings.title: Hardcoded secret committed to source
findings.description: A live-style API key (type: sk_live_* secret key) is hardcoded in source. Value redacted per policy.
findings.impact: Anyone with repo access obtains the credential; treat as compromised.
findings.evidence: Code review: a string literal of secret type at line 12. Value not reproduced here.
findings.remediation: Load from environment/secret manager (see hardened/server.js: process.env.API_KEY) and ROTATE the exposed key.

findings.title: Weak password hashing (unsalted SHA1)
findings.description: Passwords are hashed with unsalted SHA1, which is fast and trivially reversible via rainbow tables / GPU cracking.
findings.impact: Disclosed hashes (see F-002) are effectively plaintext.
findings.evidence: Code review: crypto.createHash('sha1') with no salt.
findings.remediation: Use argon2id (≥19 MiB, 2 iterations, 1 thread) or bcrypt (cost ≥10). See hardened/server.js (argon2id).

findings.title: Reflected XSS in GET /search
findings.description: The q query parameter is reflected into the HTML response without encoding.
findings.impact: Script execution in a victim's browser (session theft, actions on their behalf).
findings.evidence: Live: GET /search?q=<script>alert(1)</script> reflected the payload unescaped in the response body.
findings.remediation: Contextually HTML-encode output (see hardened/server.js escapeHtml) and add a strict CSP.

findings.title: Missing security headers
findings.description: No CSP, HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, frame-ancestors, or related headers are set.
findings.impact: Removes defense-in-depth against XSS, clickjacking, MIME sniffing, and protocol downgrade.
findings.evidence: Live: responses returned only X-Powered-By: Express; no security headers present.
findings.remediation: Add helmet() with a strict CSP and the full header set (see hardening-playbooks §3).

findings.title: Verbose error handler leaks stack traces and file paths
findings.description: The error handler returns the full stack trace, including server file paths, to the client.
findings.impact: Information disclosure aiding further attacks (path disclosure, dependency/version hints).
findings.evidence: Live: a malformed /notes id triggered a 500 with a full <pre> stack trace including absolute file paths.
findings.remediation: Return a generic error; log details server-side only (see hardened/server.js generic 500 handler).

findings.title: No rate limiting on authentication
findings.description: The login endpoint applies no throttling or lockout.
findings.evidence: Live: repeated failed logins were all processed with no throttling.
findings.remediation: Add rate limiting + backoff/lockout (see hardened/server.js express-rate-limit, 5/min).

accessibilityMap.notes: SQLi auth bypass + no rate limit
should be admin-only; leaks password hashes
